post.content: For the first question, they asked me https://leetcode.com/problems/paint-house/. Then, they asked a follow-up question: What if the colors of the first house and the last house are also required to be different? Could someone provide an optimized solution?
for the follow-up question, we can refactor the dp part into a function, and call that function 3 times for each first choice of color. not sure how to optimize it more.
How would you solve House Robber II when you have solved House Robber I? Apply the same logic here.
how does one do the follow up? anyone know?
Extend the same dynamic programming solution we had for the base problem. Set the cost for the cases where the colors match to infinity.
